subject: [PATCH 0/2] btrfs: prevent direct reclaim during compressed readahead

reviews.author: Qu Wenruo
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Qu Wenruo requested a clearer explanation of why using the existing GFP flags will lead to direct reclaim during compressed readahead, as described in the patch's cover letter.

Reviewer Qu Wenruo expressed concerns that the patch may not be optimal, as it could lead to re-read and re-decompression of folios that belong to the same compressed extent. They suggested disabling compressed readahead completely and relying on large folios instead, which they believe is a more straightforward approach.
reviews.raw_body:
I believe it will be more convincing to explain why the current gfp 
flags is going to cause the problem you mentioned in the cover letter.

---

After reading the code, I have a feeling that, we shouldn't act on the 
behalf of MM layer to add the next few folios into the page cache.

On the other hand, with the incoming large folios, we will completely 
skip the readahead for large folios.

I know this is not optimal as the next few folios may still belong to 
the same compressed extent and will cause re-read and re-decompression.

Thus I'm wondering, for your specific workload, will disabling 
compressed ra completely and fully rely on large folios help?

If the performance is acceptable, I'd prefer to disable compressed 
readahead completely and rely on large folios instead.

(Now I understand why other fses with compression support is completely 
relying on fixed IO size)

Thanks,
Qu

reviews.author: Mark Harmstone
reviews.summary: Reviewer Mark Harmstone expressed skepticism about the proposed patch as a quick fix for a production issue seen by JP Kobryn, suggesting that reworking the whole system might be a more comprehensive solution
reviews.raw_body:
Your idea might have merit, but this is a quick fix for a problem that 
JP has seen in production. Reworking the whole thing might be the 
ultimate solution, but that's much riskier, and will take more testing, 
than the proposed change.

reviews.author: Mark Harmstone
reviews.summary: reviewer agreed that readahead should fail instead of causing problems elsewhere
reviews.raw_body:
This seems a sensible change to me. Read-ahead is speculative, so it's 
better for it to fail rather than cause problems elsewhere.

reviews.author: JP (Meta) (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about explaining why current GFP flags are problematic, agreed to add more context in the patch
reviews.raw_body:
I see that now.

Qu also had a good point about including info on why the current flags
are an issue. Some of the cover letter text would help here. I'll bring
some of that over and expand on it so we have the relevant details here.

reviews.author: David Sterba
reviews.summary: Reviewer David Sterba suggested dropping the current patch and instead extending the existing btrfs_alloc_compr_folio() function to handle GFP flags, citing that the API is internal and doesn't require fine-grained control.
reviews.raw_body:
If you still need to set the gfp flags in v2, please drop this patch and
extend btrfs_alloc_compr_folio(), the API is internal and we don't need
it fine grained.

2026-03-20.patch_summary: This patch prevents direct reclaim during compressed readahead in Btrfs file systems to reduce latency and prevent CPU stalls. When a bio is marked for readahead, it uses different GFP flags that exclude __GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM to avoid triggering direct reclaim. This change allows the system to handle memory pressure without causing long-running reclaim operations that can lead to CPU stalls.
